providerNotifications: Do not administer and consult provider if patient does not meet criteria for "minimal or no fentanyl dependence", see definition below.
Patient has any signs of opioid depedency: more than minimal depedency, need to use opioids to avoid withdrawals, unable to go without opioids.
Do not rely only on urine drug tests as they can be falsely negative; notify provider if there is suspicion of opioid dependency.

idealSteps: Vivitrol is not yet overdue. Please consult the early administration guidance (no sooner than 20 days after the last injection).
Administer Vivitrol 380 mg IM** as long as the patient meets criteria for "minimal or no fentanyl dependence".
No urine drug screen required at this interval.
**Criteria met for "minimal or no fentanyl dependence"**, see definition below
*If there is ongoing active fentanyl or other unregulated opioid use, discuss a switch to buprenorphine for more robust OUD treatment.*
**If NO intentional fentanyl use reported AND "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" criteria met**, administer Vivitrol (no UDS needed)

**If intentional fentanyl use IS reported:** Obtain a point-of-care urine drug screen (document in chart):

- UDS **Negative for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone** → Administer Vivitrol if patient meets "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" criteria.

- UDS **Positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone** → Consult provider. If history truly confirms no/minimal withdrawal and no significant fentanyl effects occur after use, may administer or consider naloxone challenge.
providerNotifications: Consult provider if the urine drug screen is positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone, or if patient does not meet "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" criteria.

idealSteps: Perform point-of-care urine drug screen
If UDS negative for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone AND criteria for "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" are met, administer Vivitrol.
If UDS positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone, do NOT administer. Further discussion needed on degree of opioid dependence. Consult provider.
providerNotifications: Consult provider if unable to obtain UDS, if UDS is positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone, or if patient does not meet "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" criteria.

idealSteps: **Consult provider before administering Vivitrol.** The injection is more than 8 weeks overdue. Provider guidance is required before proceeding.
Vivitrol is not yet overdue. Please consult the early administration guidance (no sooner than 3 weeks after the last injection).
Urine drug screen not required

**Administer Vivitrol 380 mg IM** as long as the patient has no intentional daily use of opioids. No UDS required at this interval.

If there is regular opioid use, do **NOT** administer — consult provider.
providerNotifications: Consult provider if patient has regular opioid use.

idealSteps: Obtain a point-of-care urine drug screen unless you have strong confidence the patient has not used opioids (document in chart).
If UDS negative for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone AND criteria for "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" are met, administer Vivitrol.
If UDS positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone, do NOT administer. Further discussion needed on degree of opioid dependence. Consult provider.
providerNotifications: Consult provider if UDS positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone or if there is concern about opioid dependence.

idealSteps: Obtain a point-of-care urine drug screen
If UDS negative for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone AND criteria for "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" are met, administer Vivitrol.
If UDS positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone, do NOT administer. Further discussion needed on degree of opioid dependence. Consult provider.
providerNotifications: Consult provider if unable to obtain UDS, if UDS is positive for fentanyl/methadone/opiates/oxycodone, or if patient does not meet "minimal or no fentanyl dependence" criteria.

idealSteps: **Consult provider before administering Vivitrol.** The injection is more than 8 weeks overdue. Provider guidance is required before proceeding.

requiredFields.message: Please enter the date of last Vivitrol injection.
